attempts.instruction_sent_to_studio: This site needs to be multiple pages, not a single page with anchor links. Break it into separate pages: Home (index.html), Services (services.html), About (about.html), and Contact (contact.html). Each page should be its own HTML file with proper navigation linking between them.
attempts.studio_response_summary: Classified as layout_update. Generated a build-plan card with: Internal Links (replace anchors with real page links), Home Page (remove other sections, keep only home content). Plan card showed 'Looks good, build it' button.
attempts.failure_reason: execute-plan WS message did not trigger a Claude subprocess. The plan card remained visible with no build activity in server logs. The plan approval gate failed silently — no error, no build, no response.
attempts.manual_steps_taken: Sent a second chat message: 'Rebuild the site with all 4 pages: home, services, about, contact'. Also manually updated spec.json to include all 4 pages in the pages array before this attempt.
attempts.gap_identified: execute-plan WS message handler does not trigger builds for layout_update classified requests. The plan approval is cosmetic — clicking 'Looks good' does nothing when the original message was layout_update.

attempts.instruction_sent_to_studio: Rebuild the site with all 4 pages: home, services, about, contact
attempts.studio_response_summary: Classified as 'build'. Generated a new plan card listing all 4 pages: home→index.html, services→services.html, about→about.html, contact→contact.html. Plan tagged as 'medium' scope.
attempts.failure_reason: Same plan-gate issue: execute-plan WS message sent but no build triggered. Plan card remained visible.
attempts.manual_steps_taken: Approved the plan via execute-plan WS message a second time. This time the build classifier was 'build' (not layout_update), and the Claude subprocess finally spawned.
attempts.gap_identified: execute-plan requires multiple approvals or has a race condition. First approval may be consumed by an earlier stale plan card.

attempts.studio_response_summary: Build triggered successfully. Multi-page parallel build ran. Server logs show: services.html (16506 bytes), about.html (21735 bytes), contact.html (15550 bytes) written. Post-processing: slots registered (2 across 4 pages), blueprint updated for 4 pages, logo applied to 4 pages, nav-sync across all 4, footer-sync across all 4, CSS extracted to styles.css.
attempts.manual_steps_taken: None — Studio handled the full build once the plan was approved and the build classifier was 'build'

attempts.instruction_sent_to_studio: (Verified via page tab clicks: services, about, contact)
attempts.studio_response_summary: All 4 page tabs visible in Studio (home, about, contact, services). Services page shows 'Services & Pricing' with Tarot Reading $75 — 30 min. About page shows 'About Maria' with origin story and Credentials section. Contact page shows 'Begin Your Session' with booking form fields.

prerequisite_manual_step.description: Before any Studio interaction, manually edited spec.json to change pages array from ['home'] to ['home','services','about','contact']. This was a prerequisite learned from Guy's Classy Shoes build — the build classifier reads spec.pages to determine how many pages to generate.
prerequisite_manual_step.gap_identified: spec.json pages array not updated by brief approval or new_site handler. Multi-page builds require manual spec update.

gaps_discovered: execute-plan WS handler does not trigger builds for layout_update classified requests — plan approval is cosmetic for non-build intents
Multiple plan cards accumulate in DOM — later execute-plan calls may reference wrong planId
spec.json pages array must be manually updated before multi-page build — brief approval and new_site handler only set pages=['home']
First chat message ('break into separate pages') classified as layout_update instead of build — classifier needs a 'restructure' or 'split_pages' intent
The working path was: (1) manually update spec.json pages, (2) send 'Rebuild the site with all 4 pages' which classified as 'build', (3) approve the plan twice due to stale plan card

final_evaluation.could_studio_handle_conversion: Partially. Studio successfully generated the 4-page build once the right classifier intent was triggered ('build'). But the initial 'break into pages' request was misclassified as layout_update, and the plan approval gate failed silently on the first attempt.
final_evaluation.steps_requiring_manual_intervention: spec.json pages array update (prerequisite), re-sending with 'Rebuild' wording to trigger build classifier, double plan approval due to stale plan cards
final_evaluation.classifier_analysis: 'Break into separate pages' → layout_update (wrong). 'Rebuild the site with all 4 pages' → build (correct). The classifier has no 'restructure' or 'page_split' intent — requests to change site architecture route to layout_update which tries to modify the current page.
final_evaluation.gap_location: Three separate gaps: (1) Classifier — no restructure intent, (2) Spec updater — brief/new_site don't populate pages array from design_brief.must_have_sections, (3) Plan approval — execute-plan handler has race condition or silent failure when layout_update generates a plan
final_evaluation.what_would_fix_this: 1. Add spec.pages auto-population from design_brief.must_have_sections during brief approval. 2. Add 'restructure' classifier intent that triggers full rebuild. 3. Fix execute-plan handler to actually spawn Claude for all plan types, not just 'build'.
